shortDescription: German Embassy · Points-based job search · 1 year · No job offer required

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current Opportunity Card points requirements and blocked account amount on make-it-in-germany.com and embassy website before quoting.

alert.title: Points-based eligibility
alert.body: Opportunity Card requires minimum 6 points from qualifications, experience, language, and age. Verify points calculation before application.

about.label: Description
about.value: Residence permit for qualified professionals to search for employment in Germany for up to one year without prior job offer. Points-based eligibility under Section 20a AufenthG.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Minimum 6 points · Recognised qualification or IT experience · German A1 or English B2 · Blocked account or employment contract · Age typically under 35 for max age points

about.label: Points system
about.value: Points for qualification recognition, work experience, language, age, and previous Germany stay. Minimum 6 points required.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: German Embassy in India · Local Ausländerbehörde after arrival

about.label: After approval
about.value: Enter Germany; search for qualified employment; convert to work residence permit when job secured.

eligibility.criterion: Recognised degree or IT experience pathway

redFlagsBanner: If below 6 points, improve language level or obtain qualification recognition before applying—do not submit ineligible application.

redFlags.title: Below 6 points
redFlags.description: Application lodged without meeting minimum points.
redFlags.fix: Calculate points honestly; improve language or get recognition

redFlags.title: Qualification not recognised
redFlags.description: Degree not equivalent to German qualification for points.
redFlags.fix: Check Anabin database; apply for recognition if needed

redFlags.title: Insufficient blocked account
redFlags.description: Funds below required amount for stay.
redFlags.fix: Open blocked account for required duration

redFlags.title: Language certificate missing
redFlags.description: Neither German A1 nor English B2 provided.

redFlags.title: Wrong visa category
redFlags.description: Applied Opportunity Card when job offer exists—work visa may be better.
redFlags.fix: Assess job offer vs job search pathway at intake

redFlags.title: Incomplete IT experience evidence
redFlags.description: IT pathway without 2 years documented experience.
redFlags.fix: Detailed employer letters for IT experience route

faqs.q: Can we guarantee Opportunity Card approval?
faqs.a: No. Embassy assesses points and documentation. Never promise approval.
Use official criteria on make-it-in-germany.com—qualification, experience, language, age, prior Germany stay.
No—that is the purpose. Search for job after arrival. Blocked account required instead.
Limited—part-time work while searching allowed per current rules. Verify limits.
Opportunity Card replaces old job seeker for points-based pathway—verify current rules.
Verify current amount on embassy website—similar to student blocked account.
Apply for work residence permit at Ausländerbehörde.
No strict limit but fewer age points above 35.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee visa or immigration approval; use approved-language only
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, and third-party costs
proTips: Calculate 6 points at intake before fees
Obtain B2 English or A1 German early
Open blocked account when file ready
Prepare CV in German and English formats
postApproval: Advise on converting to work permit when employed

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Initial Germany Opportunity Card counselor content.

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Points calculation worksheet mandatory sign-off before embassy appointment booking.

resources.title: Make it in Germany — Opportunity Card
Make it in Germany — Points system

quiz.question: Purpose of Opportunity Card:
options: Job search in Germany up to 1 year

dos: Obtain A1 German or B2 English
donts: Do not use unrecognised degrees without IT route
Do not quote outdated points criteria
